meta.name: Soul Food Fried Chicken
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog

source.notes: Canonical SSA soul food baseline. Designed for reliable home execution with cast iron or deep fryer.

meta.summary: Classic soul food fried chicken with buttermilk hot-sauce marinade, seasoned flour dredge, and crisp golden crust.

notes: Cook chicken to 165°F internal temperature in the thickest part (avoid bone).
Do not reuse raw marinade as a sauce unless boiled.

ingredients.label: Chicken pieces (drumsticks, thighs, wings, breasts cut in half if large), skin-on, bone-in
Neutral frying oil (peanut/canola/vegetable) — enough for 1/2–3/4 inch depth

steps.title: Prep chicken
steps.body: Pat chicken dry with paper towels. Trim any excess loose skin/fat if desired.

steps.title: Make buttermilk marinade
steps.body: Whisk buttermilk, hot sauce, kosher salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika until smooth and evenly combined.

steps.title: Marinate (cold)
steps.body: Add chicken to marinade, turn to coat, cover, and refrigerate at least 4 hours (8–12 hours preferred).

steps.title: Warm slightly before frying
steps.body: Remove chicken from refrigerator 20–30 minutes before frying (keep it in marinade while it warms slightly).

steps.title: Mix seasoned dredge
steps.body: Combine flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, and optional cayenne/thyme in a wide bowl.

steps.title: Dredge chicken for craggy crust
steps.body: Lift chicken from marinade, let excess drip off, then press firmly into seasoned flour on all sides. Optional: drizzle 2–3 tbsp of marinade into the flour, toss to form small clumps, then press chicken again into those clumps.

steps.title: Rest dredged chicken
steps.body: Place dredged chicken on a wire rack over a sheet pan and rest 10–15 minutes so the coating hydrates and adheres.

steps.title: Heat oil
steps.body: Heat oil in a cast iron skillet to 325–350°F (aim ~335°F). Use a thermometer to monitor temperature.

steps.title: Fry in batches
steps.body: Lower chicken into oil (skin-side down when applicable). Fry without crowding; adjust heat to keep oil between 325–350°F.

steps.title: Flip and cook through
steps.body: Turn pieces every 3–5 minutes for even browning. Typical totals: thighs/drums 12–16 min, wings 8–12 min, breast halves 12–18 min depending on size and oil temp.

steps.title: Confirm doneness
steps.body: Check thickest part with an instant thermometer: minimum 165°F internal (170–175°F ideal for thighs/drums).

steps.title: Drain and serve
steps.body: Transfer to a wire rack over a sheet pan to drain and stay crisp. Serve hot (optional: a tiny pinch of fine salt right after frying).
